title: Paraderris elliptica
taxonomy: Paraderris elliptica is classified within the family Annonaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the custard-apple family. Paraderris elliptica is further classified within the genus Paraderris, which is a part of the order Magnoliales. The order Magnoliales is a group of flowering plants that also includes the magnolia and sassafras families. Paraderris elliptica is native to Australia and is a member of the flowering plant division Magnoliophyta.
uses: Paraderris elliptica has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. It is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various conditions including fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. However, further research is needed to confirm its medicinal effectiveness.
